content.text: Found it — the pod command has a special-case for `crdb-cluster-1` that binds its HTTP server to `127.0.0.1:8080` instead of `0.0.0.0:8080`, making it unreachable for kubelet's health probes. Let me check the StatefulSet definition.

input.command: kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset -o name; echo "---"; kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset crdb-cluster -o yaml | grep -n -A 30 "command:"

content.text: This confirms the bug: the container command binds the HTTP server to `127.0.0.1:8080` for `crdb-cluster-1` specifically, so kubelet's liveness/readiness probes (which hit the pod IP) can never reach it. I'll patch the StatefulSet to always bind to `0.0.0.0:8080`.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb get statefulset crdb-cluster -o json | python3 -c "
import json,sys
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
c = d['spec']['template']['spec']['containers'][0]
print(c['command'])
"

content.content:
Exit code 127
/bin/bash: line 7: python3: command not found

tool_use_result:
Error: Exit code 127
/bin/bash: line 7: python3: command not found

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' > /tmp/new-command.json
[
  "/bin/bash",
  "-c",
  "exec /cockroach/cockroach start --logtostderr=INFO --insecure --advertise-host=$(POD_NAME).crdb-cluster.$(POD_NAMESPACE).svc.cluster.local --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 --port=26257 --cache=25% --max-sql-memory=25% --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.$(POD_NAMESPACE).svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.$(POD_NAMESPACE).svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.$(POD_NAMESPACE).svc.cluster.local:26257"
]
EOF
kubectl -n cockroachdb patch statefulset crdb-cluster --type=json -p "[{\"op\":\"replace\",\"path\":\"/spec/template/spec/containers/0/command\",\"value\":$(cat /tmp/new-command.json)}]"

content.text: Now I need to restart `crdb-cluster-1` (and confirm the others aren't affected) so it picks up the new command.

content.text: The StatefulSet template is updated. Now I'll delete the unhealthy `crdb-cluster-1` pod so it gets recreated with the fixed command (a StatefulSet update alone doesn't restart running pods unless using RollingUpdate triggers, but deleting forces recreation with the new template).

content.content: Command running in background with ID: ba19uvc49. Output is being written to: /tmp/claude-0/-workspace/5a625900-f2bd-4161-88d0-3bc85338d135/tasks/ba19uvc49.output. You will be notified when it completes. To check interim output, use Read on that file path.

content.text: I'll wait for the rollout status command to complete since I already backgrounded it.

input.command: cat /tmp/claude-0/-workspace/5a625900-f2bd-4161-88d0-3bc85338d135/tasks/ba19uvc49.output; until ! kill -0 $(pgrep -f "rollout status statefulset/crdb-cluster" | head -1) 2>/dev/null; do sleep 3; done; echo DONE; cat /tmp/claude-0/-workspace/5a625900-f2bd-4161-88d0-3bc85338d135/tasks/ba19uvc49.output

content.text: The StatefulSet update triggered a rolling restart of all three pods (since the template changed), so they're all cycling now. Let me wait for them to stabilize.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -w &
WPID=$!
sleep 90
kill $WPID 2>/dev/null
echo "---final---"
kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods

description:
